title: verbascum rubricaule
intro: Verbascum rubricaule, also known as the Crimson Mullein, is a biennial plant species within the Mullein genus, characterized by its striking crimson or reddish-purple flowers and fuzzy, velvety leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Verbascum rubricaule include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, Verbascum rubricaule is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
